architecture.summary: Migrate react-grid-layout to React 19 and dnd-kit v0.3.2: rewrite interactive components to use DndContext/useSortable, move pure core logic into new core, and add tests for DnD correctness.

src/react/components/GridItem.tsx.notes: Replace react-draggable/react-resizable usage with dnd-kit useSortable and DragOverlay. Remove forwardRef patterns; expose ref as prop. Map visual state (dragging,resizing) to useActionState/useTransition. component_analysis: src/react/components/GridItem.tsx (visual state dragging,resizing; deps react-draggable,react-resizable).

src/react/components/GridLayout.tsx.notes: Introduce DndContext with PointerSensor/KeyboardSensor and grid collision strategy; use useSortable for children; remove internal imperative drag handlers and rely on DnD kit events. component_analysis: src/react/components/GridLayout.tsx (logical state mounted,layout; visual activeDrag,resizing).

src/react/components/ResponsiveGridLayou.notes: Wrap GridLayout changes, synchronizeLayoutWithChildren remains pure but must be adapted for new hook outputs. component_analysis: ResponsiveGridLayout (breakpoint,cols,layout,layouts).

src/react/components/WidthProvider.tsx.notes: Modernize to React 19 patterns: functional component, use() when reading async resources if needed. No DnD-specific code but used by legacy wrappers. component_analysis: WidthProvider (width,mounted).

src/react/components/index.ts.notes: Re-export updated components from new dnd-react-layout src/react. Keep exports relative and avoid imports from legacy src/.

src/react/hooks/useGridLayout.ts.notes: Split responsibilities into composable hooks: useGridState (logical layout), useDnDIntegration (visual dragState/resizeState) using useActionState/useTransition and DndContext hooks. component_analysis: useGridLayout (layout,dropState; visual dragState,resizeState).

src/react/hooks/useResponsiveLayout.ts.notes: Adapt to new core layout API surface; keep pure use() semantics for breakpoint/layout resolution. component_analysis: useResponsiveLayout (breakpoint,cols,layouts).

src/react/hooks/useContainerWidth.ts.notes: Switch to React 19 patterns for measuring container width; prefer use() for synchronous resources when available. component_analysis: useContainerWidth (width,mounted).

src/core/collision.ts.notes: Keep pure functions but adapt signatures for dnd-kit collision strategies; expose helper functions for modifiers (grid snapping).

src/core/layout.ts.notes: Expose a compatibility shim for existing API while gradually replacing internals; ensure calculate/layout/compactors compatibility.

src/core/compactors.ts.notes: Refactor compaction algorithms into pure functions suitable for reuse by DnD handlers; preserve algorithmic behavior (use tests from contracts).

src/core/calculate.ts.notes: Preserve drop alignment calculations (see truth_tables PR #2167). Ensure functions are pure and accept grid metrics from new hooks.

src/core/position.ts.notes: Pure geometry helpers; migrate to dnd-react-layout/src/core.

src/core/constraints.ts.notes: Constraint enforcement logic kept pure and reused by hooks.

src/core/types.ts.notes: Define TS types for new workspace; ensure compatibility layer for older consumers.

globalSteps.description: Audit and freeze pure core functions in src/core. Create dnd-react-layout/src/core package surface and copy/rewire pure functions (collision,calculate,compactors,layout,position,constraints,types).
Design composable hooks: useGridState, useDnDIntegration, useResponsiveLayout, useContainerWidth. Each hook does one responsibility and uses React 19 patterns (use(), useTransition/useActionState).

Rewrite GridItem, GridLayout, ResponsiveGridLayout to functional components that use dnd-kit (DndContext, useSortable, DragOverlay) and new hooks. Remove react-draggable/react-resizable imports. Ensure no forwardRef usage.
Create DndProvider wrapper configuring PointerSensor and KeyboardSensor, collision strategies from @dnd-kit/collision, and modifiers for grid-snapping. Implement DragOverlay placeholder.
Provide compatibility shims in src/core to maintain older API while clients migrate. Mark shims for later removal.
Add unit tests for core math and compaction, and integration tests for dnd interactions validating truth table cases (drop alignment, column width).
Create AST patches per file and move to IR.patches.planned; iterate with small pull requests. Run CI and update contracts.

testingPlan: Unit tests: core math (collision,calculate,compactors,position,constraints) — pure deterministic tests ported from existing test/spec/*. Ensure behaviour unchanged.
Hook tests: mount hooks with React Test Renderer and simulate dnd-kit events where possible. Validate state transitions with useActionState/useTransition.

Integration tests: shallow render GridLayout+GridItem under DndContext; simulate drag start/move/drop using dnd-kit utilities; assert resulting layout and drop alignment matches truth_tables (PR #2167).
E2E (optional): run a headless browser scenario to validate dragging and resizing flows end-to-end for critical examples (01-basic, 12-drag-from-outside).

dnd_mapping.note: Full DnD mathematical model stored at IR.dnd_analysis. Extract performed by orchestrator direct-analysis from src/core/calculate.ts, collision.ts, compactors.ts, constraints.ts.

summary.dndkitTarget: @dnd-kit/dom Draggable + PointerSensor + snapToGrid modifier + @dnd-kit/collision AABB adapter + DragOverlay

gridPixelSteps.formula: stepX = calcGridColWidth(positionParams) + margin[0]; stepY = rowHeight + margin[1]

gridUnitToPixelSize.formula: px = round(colOrRowSize * gridUnits + max(0, gridUnits - 1) * marginPx)
edgeCases: If gridUnits is not finite, return gridUnits unchanged.
This is used for both width and height.

formula.width: width = resizePosition ? round(resizePosition.width) : calcGridItemWHPx(w, colWidth, margin[0])
formula.height: height = resizePosition ? round(resizePosition.height) : calcGridItemWHPx(h, rowHeight, margin[1])

overrides.drag: If dragPosition exists, top = round(dragPosition.top) and left = round(dragPosition.left).
overrides.resize: If resizePosition exists, top = round(resizePosition.top), left = round(resizePosition.left), width = round(resizePosition.width), height = round(resizePosition.height).

formula.horizontal: siblingLeft = round((colWidth + margin[0]) * (x + w) + containerPadding[0]); actualMarginRight = siblingLeft - left - width; if actualMarginRight !== margin[0], width += actualMarginRight - margin[0]
formula.vertical: siblingTop = round((rowHeight + margin[1]) * (y + h) + containerPadding[1]); actualMarginBottom = siblingTop - top - height; if actualMarginBottom !== margin[1], height += actualMarginBottom - margin[1]

roundingMarginCorrection.appliesWhen: Only when neither dragPosition nor resizePosition is active.

pixelToGridClamped.usedNow: GridLayout external dropping placeholder path uses calcXY.

pixelToGridRaw.usedNow: Current GridItem drag flow converts pointer-driven left/top to raw grid units, then applies constraints via applyPositionConstraints.

formula.defaultClamp: _w = clamp(w, 0, cols - x); _h = clamp(h, 0, maxRows - y)

pixelToGridSizeRaw.usedNow: Current GridItem resize flow uses calcWHRaw first, then applySizeConstraints.

formula.startLeft: left0 = clientRect.left / transformScale - parentRect.left / transformScale + offsetParent.scrollLeft
formula.startTop: top0 = clientRect.top / transformScale - parentRect.top / transformScale + offsetParent.scrollTop

drag.formula: deltaX = (coords.clientX - lastClientX) / transformScale; deltaY = (coords.clientY - lastClientY) / transformScale

resize.formula: deltaX = (coords.clientX - startClientX) / transformScale; deltaY = (coords.clientY - startClientY) / transformScale

notes: Current native pointer math is already scale-aware.
Current drag origin is offsetParent-relative and scroll-aware.

formula.clamp: clampedGridX = max(0, rawGridX); clampedGridY = max(0, rawGridY)
formula.droppingPosition: left = clampedGridX / transformScale; top = clampedGridY / transformScale

boundsHelper.formula: bounds = { left: x, right: x + w, top: y, bottom: y + h }

formula.expression: collision = !(bounds.right <= item.x || bounds.left >= item.x + item.w || bounds.bottom <= item.y || bounds.top >= item.y + item.h)
formula.intervalModel: Half-open overlap on both axes; touching edges are not collisions.

first.algorithm: Linear scan, first item where collides(item, layoutItem) is true.

formula: If hasCollisions && preventCollision => restore oldX, oldY, moved=false and return original layout reference.
Else resolve each collision through moveElementAwayFromCollision.
drag.note: dnd-kit should not replace this reducer; it should only feed candidate x/y.

resize.formula: If preventCollision && !allowOverlap and getAllCollisions(currentLayout, proposedItem).filter(i => i.i !== layoutItem.i).length > 0, then restore original x,y,w,h and cancel resize move.
resize.note: Resize collision blocking currently lives in GridLayout, not in core/collision.ts.

formula: projectedTarget = axis === 'x' ? projectedItem.x + projectedItem.w : projectedItem.y + projectedItem.h.
For later items in sorted layout: skip undefined and static items; if !layoutHasStatics && otherItem.y > item.y + item.h, break early.
If collides(item, otherItem), recursively move otherItem to projectedTarget on the same axis.
edgeCases: Static items disable the early-break assumption.
The temporary +1 before scanning prevents missing immediate chain collisions.

sortOrder.formula: Sort by y ascending, then x ascending.

formula: while y > 0 and no collision with compareWith: y--
while collision exists: resolveCompactionCollision(fullLayout, item, collision.y + collision.h, 'y')

vertical.layoutAlgorithm: compareWith starts as static items; maxY starts as bottom(compareWith); each non-static item is compacted, pushed into compareWith, and maxY becomes max(maxY, y + h).

sortOrder.formula: Sort by x ascending, then y ascending.

formula: while x > 0 and no collision with compareWith: x--
while collision exists: resolveCompactionCollision(fullLayout, item, collision.x + collision.w, 'x')
if x + w > cols: x = cols - w; y++; then while x > 0 and no collision with compareWith: x--

edgeCases: Horizontal overflow wraps item to the next row before retrying left compaction.

none.algorithm: compact(layout) returns cloneLayout(layout); no gap filling, no automatic movement.

position.formula: Nearest-cell snap comes from Math.round((pixel - padding) / gridStep).

size.formula: Nearest-size snap comes from Math.round((pixelSize + margin) / gridStepSize).

currentImplicitSnap.note: Current drag/resize sessions do not snap pointer deltas in pixel space first; they convert current left/top or width/height to grid units by rounding.

explicitGridUnitSnapConstraint.note: This is magnetism in grid-unit space, not pixel space.

pixelSnapUtility.bestDndKitUse: Use as a dnd-kit modifier for visual drag overlay snapping while keeping final calcXYRaw/applyPositionConstraints as the source of truth.

position.formula: Grid-level constraints run in array order, then item.constraints in array order.

size.formula: Grid-level constraints run first, then item.constraints.

sizeFormula.maxW: maxW = handle in ['w','nw','sw'] ? item.x + item.w : cols - item.x
sizeFormula.maxH: maxH = handle in ['n','nw','ne'] ? item.y + item.h : maxRows - item.y

formula.visibleRows: visibleRows = containerHeight > 0 ? floor((containerHeight + margin[1]) / (rowHeight + margin[1])) : maxRows

containerBounds.currentCodebaseNote: GridItem builds ConstraintContext with containerHeight: 0, so this currently falls back to maxRows unless a different caller supplies a real height.

axisLock.currentState: No dedicated drag axis prop is wired into current GridItem native sessions.

availableConstraints.formula: x = clamp(x, 0, max(0, cols - item.w)); y unchanged
y = clamp(y, 0, max(0, maxRows - item.h)); x unchanged

axisLock.mappingNote: For dnd-kit, axis lock should live in modifiers or sensor policy, while current constraint functions remain valid as grid-unit clamps.

formula.pixelWidth: pixelWidth = colWidth * w + margin[0] * max(0, w - 1)

edgeCases: This implementation explicitly ignores containerPadding in colWidth.
Aspect ratio is enforced in pixel space and converted back to grid units.
This is pixel-space DOM bounding, distinct from gridBounds.
It ignores containerPadding in the final clamp and uses rendered width/height.

formula.north: top = currentSize.top - (height - currentSize.height); top = max(0, top); if top < 0 then height reverts to currentHeight
formula.west: left = currentSize.left + currentSize.width - width; if left < 0 then left = 0 and width = currentSize.left + currentSize.width
formula.east: if currentSize.left + newWidth > containerWidth then width reverts to currentWidth
formula.south: top is clamped to >= 0 and negative-top growth is rejected

resizeDirectionNormalization.note: Current resize flow first creates a candidate from pointer delta, then normalizes it per handle direction, then converts to grid units via calcWHRaw and applySizeConstraints.

replaceNativePointerSessions.replaceWith: @dnd-kit/react DndContext sensors plus @dnd-kit/dom Draggable per GridItem
replaceNativePointerSessions.preserve: Keep calcXYRaw -> applyPositionConstraints -> GridLayout/useGridLayout callbacks as the source of truth for grid x/y.

replaceNativePointerSessions.replaceWith: Handle-level dnd-kit draggables or a dedicated resize sensor abstraction
replaceNativePointerSessions.preserve: Keep createResizeCandidate -> resizeItemInDirection -> calcWHRaw -> applySizeConstraints -> GridLayout.onResize.

dragPipeline.dndKitEntry: @dnd-kit/react DndContext with PointerSensor and KeyboardSensor; @dnd-kit/dom Draggable for each non-static item
mathMapping: Use dnd-kit pointer delta, divide by transformScale exactly as current GridItem does.
Reconstruct parent-relative candidate left/top using the current offsetParent-relative start position model from getDragStartPosition.
Convert candidate left/top with calcXYRaw, then applyPositionConstraints.
Pass constrained x/y into existing GridLayout.onDrag and useGridLayout.onDrag so moveElement and compaction remain unchanged.

fastDrag.mapping: PointerSensor activationConstraint.distance should default to 3 to match current drag threshold semantics.

resizePipeline.dndKitEntry: Use dedicated dnd-kit draggables for resize handles rather than replacing core resize math.
mathMapping: Handle delta stays pixel-based and scale-normalized.
Convert pixel size to raw grid units with calcWHRaw.
Delegate to existing GridLayout.onResize and GridLayout.onResizeStop for collision rollback and compaction.
resizePipeline.whyNotReplaceMath: Current handle-aware north/west edge behavior and collision rollback are already encoded in GridItem.tsx, src/core/position.ts, and GridLayout.tsx; dnd-kit should only provide interaction events.

adapterPlan: Build collision candidates from current layout items in grid space, not from DOM rectangles alone.
Use collides/collidesBounds exactly for overlap semantics.
Exclude static items from draggable/sortable registration, but keep them in collision candidate data so they still block or push layouts.
Do not resolve movement inside the dnd-kit strategy; return collision information only.
Keep preventCollision and allowOverlap semantics in moveElement and GridLayout.onResize.

plan: Visual snapping should use pixel steps from getGridPixelSteps or snapPixelDeltaToGrid so the overlay tracks cell boundaries.
Committed layout coordinates should still be computed by calcXYRaw/calcWHRaw and constraints, not by trusting modifier output alone.
If a caller uses constraints.snapToGrid(stepX, stepY), keep that in the grid-unit constraint chain.
Replace the visual floating dragged node with DragOverlay.
Keep dragState.activeDrag in useGridLayout as the occupancy placeholder used by layout callbacks and compaction.
Render overlay position using the same createStyle/calcGridItemPosition conventions so transform/top-left behavior stays aligned with current code.

scrollContainer.mapping: Preserve offsetParent.scrollLeft/scrollTop compensation when deriving the drag origin; do not switch to viewport-only coordinates.

scaleTransform.mapping: All dnd-kit deltas must be divided by transformScale before calcXYRaw/calcWHRaw; otherwise scaled containers drift.

staticItems.mapping: Static items must remain in layout math and collision data, but must not become draggable/sortable sources.

replaceNow: Manual pointer/mouse/touch event registration in GridItem
keepNow: GridLayout external HTML5 drop flow in handleDragOver/handleDrop
GridLayout placeholder state for occupancy and layout callbacks

implementationBatch1.rationale: Batch 1 should replace only the interaction transport layer with dnd-kit while leaving the existing grid math, collision reducers, and compaction reducers untouched.
implementationBatch1.hookStrategy: Introduce a dnd-kit-backed drag/resize orchestration layer at the React boundary that emits the same onDragStart/onDrag/onDragStop/onResizeStart/onResize/onResizeStop contracts already consumed by GridLayout and useGridLayout.
implementationBatch1.overlayStrategy: Use DragOverlay for the floating dragged preview, but keep dragState.activeDrag as the in-grid placeholder state that still drives occupancy, callbacks, and compaction visuals.
implementationBatch1.resizeStrategy: Model each resize handle as its own dnd-kit draggable interaction and continue routing handle deltas through createResizeCandidate, resizeItemInDirection, calcWHRaw, applySizeConstraints, and GridLayout.onResize.
implementationBatch1.collisionAdapterStrategy: Add a custom @dnd-kit/collision adapter that reads the current layout and mirrors collides/collidesBounds semantics; do not embed moveElement, preventCollision, or compaction inside the collision strategy itself.
risks: Overlay and measured node may diverge under transformScale if dnd-kit deltas are not normalized exactly like current native sessions.
Offset-parent-relative origin plus scrollLeft/scrollTop parity must be preserved or dragged items will drift in scrolled containers.
Static items must remain collision participants even though they cannot be draggable/sortable sources.
Current .react-resizable-handle cancel behavior must be preserved or drag and resize sensors will fight.
Switching containerBounds to a real containerHeight later may intentionally change current behavior because GridItem currently passes containerHeight: 0.
Resize remains custom even after drag migrates, so interaction state ownership between drag overlay and resize handles must stay mutually exclusive.

next_dnd_phase.summary: La prochaine vague doit supprimer le dernier point dur legacy dans GridItem.tsx (imports react-draggable/react-resizable) et réaligner GridLayout/useGridLayout sur un flux @dnd-kit v0.3.2 sans toucher au noyau mathématique déjà factorisé dans calculate/collision/compactors/constraints.

remainingLegacyDnD.legacyPattern: ImportDeclaration[source='react-draggable'] at line 16 and ImportDeclaration[source='react-resizable'] at line 17; JSXElement#DraggableCore wraps the item at lines 738-751 and JSXElement#Resizable wraps it at lines 719-735. Drag callbacks consume DraggableCore deltaX/deltaY in onDrag (lines 312-410), scale compensation uses transformScale in onDragStart/on wrapper scale prop (lines 239-242, 747), bounds are clamped in pixels when isBounded=true (lines 358-371), and droppingPosition replays fake DraggableCore payloads via useEffect (lines 587-655).
remainingLegacyDnD.dndKitTarget: @dnd-kit/react DndContext + DragOverlay, @dnd-kit/dom draggable hooks/sensors, @dnd-kit/abstract modifier chain for snap/axis/bounds, custom pointer-driven resize handles feeding existing calcWHRaw/applySizeConstraints pipeline
migrationSteps: Replace DraggableCore event dependence with pointer delta from dnd-kit sensors, then compute pixel position exactly as today: dragPositionRef.current += delta/transformScale before calcXYRaw and applyPositionConstraints.
Preserve selector semantics from handle/cancel by mapping them to activator nodes or activation guards; keep static items non-draggable/non-resizable.

Keep pixel-bounds behavior before grid conversion: when isBounded=true, clamp top to [0, offsetParent.clientHeight - calcGridItemWHPx(h,rowHeight,margin[1])] and left to [0, containerWidth - calcGridItemWHPx(w,calcGridColWidth(positionParams),margin[0])].

Replace Resizable with explicit resize handles that compute updatedSize through the same directional math, then call calcWHRaw -> applySizeConstraints; preserve north/west handle position shifts and transformScale compensation.
Replace droppingPosition fake drag replay with dnd-kit overlay state updates; do not synthesize legacy deltaX/deltaY objects once GridLayout owns active drag state in DndContext.
Keep visual position rendering on calcGridItemPosition, including the sibling-gap rounding correction branch when not dragging/resizing.

remainingLegacyDnD.legacyPattern: GridLayout no longer imports legacy libs directly, but its interaction contract is still shaped around GridItem's DraggableCore/Resizable callbacks: activeDrag placeholder state is inline React state (lines 303-306, 823-851), drag/resize lifecycles assume start/drag/stop callback cadence from child wrappers (lines 333-504), and external drop currently injects a temporary layout item plus droppingPosition for GridItem to simulate drag (lines 535-714, 879).
remainingLegacyDnD.dndKitTarget: @dnd-kit/react DndContext at grid root, custom @dnd-kit/collision strategy backed by core/collision.ts, DragOverlay for placeholder/preview, sensors configured for pointer+keyboard, and modifiers using core/calculate.ts/constraints.ts
migrationSteps: Make GridLayout the DndContext host and move drag lifecycle ownership here; GridItem should become a consumer of active/over/transform state rather than source of legacy callback payloads.

Use a custom collision strategy that evaluates layout items in grid units with collides()/getAllCollisions() semantics and honors preventCollision/allowOverlap exactly as current moveElement + compactor flow does.

Replace inline placeholder rendering with DragOverlay while still updating activeDrag grid coordinates for compaction preview; keep public onLayoutChange filtered from transient dropping placeholder state.

Port handleDragOver/handleDrop to dnd-kit droppable measurements but preserve exact external-drop math: derive clampedGridX/Y from pointer relative to gridRect, center by item pixel size, divide by transformScale, then calcXY(positionParams, top, left, w, h).
Preserve compactor.compact() after move/resize/drop stop; do not change vertical/horizontal/no-compactor semantics.
Map dragThreshold to sensor activationConstraint with a minimum distance of 3 to avoid accidental drags while still supporting existing threshold prop.

remainingLegacyDnD.legacyPattern: The hook is library-agnostic but assumes legacy-style discrete callbacks onDragStart/onDrag/onDragStop and onResizeStart/onResize/onResizeStop that receive already-snapped grid units (lines 81-107, 164-252). It also keeps activeDrag as a placeholder item rather than an overlay descriptor.
remainingLegacyDnD.dndKitTarget: State reducer backing @dnd-kit/react DndContext, where drag sensor deltas are converted to grid units before invoking the existing moveElement/compactor pipeline
migrationSteps: Keep the layout mutation core, but refactor the hook boundary so DndContext drives it with active id, pointer delta, and resize handle metadata instead of legacy child callbacks.
Use calcXY/calcWH or calcXYRaw/calcWHRaw plus constraint application before calling moveElement/onResize to maintain identical snapping and bounds behavior.
Separate overlay state from committed layout state so fast pointer movement and external drop previews do not depend on legacy placeholder semantics.
Retain preventCollision, compactor.type, and compactor.allowOverlap behavior unchanged.

coordinateTransforms: dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcGridColWidth => colWidth = (containerWidth - margin[0] * (cols - 1) - containerPadding[0] * 2) / cols; use as dnd-kit horizontal step basis stepX = colWidth + margin[0].

dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcGridItemWHPx => sizePx = round(unitSize * gridUnits + max(0, gridUnits - 1) * marginPx); width uses colWidth/margin[0], height uses rowHeight/margin[1].

dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcGridItemPosition => top = round((rowHeight + margin[1]) * y + containerPadding[1]), left = round((colWidth + margin[0]) * x + containerPadding[0]); if dragPosition exists, left/top are exact drag pixels; if resizePosition exists, width/height and optionally left/top are exact resize pixels; when neither drag nor resize is active, width/height are margin-corrected by siblingStart - start - size to preserve exact gap after rounding.

dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcXY => x = round((left - containerPadding[0]) / (colWidth + margin[0])), y = round((top - containerPadding[1]) / (rowHeight + margin[1])), then clamp x to [0, cols - w] and y to [0, maxRows - h].
dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcXYRaw => same formula as calcXY without clamp; this is the correct dnd-kit pre-constraint conversion path for drag.

dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcWH => w = round((width + margin[0]) / (colWidth + margin[0])), h = round((height + margin[1]) / (rowHeight + margin[1])); clamp by handle direction so west handles can expand to cols and north handles to maxRows.

dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcWHRaw => raw resize conversion uses the same rounding formula but enforces minimum 1; this is the correct dnd-kit pre-constraint conversion path for resize.
snapRules: dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#calcXY, FunctionDeclaration#calcXYRaw, FunctionDeclaration#calcWH, FunctionDeclaration#calcWHRaw all snap with Math.round to nearest whole grid unit; dnd-kit modifiers must preserve nearest-cell rounding, not floor/ceil.

dnd-react-layout/src/core/calculate.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#snapPixelDeltaToGrid and FunctionDeclaration#getGridPixelSteps => stepX = calcGridColWidth(positionParams) + margin[0], stepY = rowHeight + margin[1], snappedDelta = round(delta / step) * step; this is the exact pixel-grid magnetism model for sensor deltas.

dnd-react-layout/src/core/constraints.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#snapToGrid => x' = round(x / stepX) * stepX, y' = round(y / stepY) * stepY; if axis lock is needed in dnd-kit, combine this with VariableDeclarator#boundedX or VariableDeclarator#boundedY rather than changing core math.
collisionRules: dnd-react-layout/src/core/collision.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#collides via FunctionDeclaration#collidesBounds implements AABB in grid units: no collision if right1 <= left2 or left1 >= right2 or bottom1 <= top2 or top1 >= bottom2; otherwise collision; self-collision short-circuits when l1.i === l2.i.

dnd-react-layout/src/core/collision.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#getFirstCollision scans layout in order and returns first overlapping item; this ordering matters for compaction and should be preserved in any custom dnd-kit collision adapter.

dnd-react-layout/src/core/collision.ts :: FunctionDeclaration#getAllCollisions returns every overlapping layout item; GridLayout resize logic uses this to revert resize when preventCollision && !allowOverlap.

next_dnd_phase.notes: Ne pas modifier le noyau mathématique: dnd-kit doit appeler les fonctions existantes, pas les réécrire. Références AST à préserver explicitement dans la migration: FunctionDeclaration#calcGridColWidth, FunctionDeclaration#calcGridItemWHPx, FunctionDeclaration#calcGridItemPosition, FunctionDeclaration#calcXY, FunctionDeclaration#calcXYRaw, FunctionDeclaration#calcWH, FunctionDeclaration#calcWHRaw, FunctionDeclaration#collides, FunctionDeclaration#getFirstCollision, FunctionDeclaration#getAllCollisions, FunctionDeclaration#resolveCompactionCollision, FunctionDeclaration#compactItemVertical, FunctionDeclaration#compactItemHorizontal, VariableDeclarator#verticalCompactor, VariableDeclarator#horizontalCompactor, VariableDeclarator#noCompactor, VariableDeclarator#gridBounds, VariableDeclarator#minMaxSize, VariableDeclarator#boundedX, VariableDeclarator#boundedY, FunctionDeclaration#aspectRatio, FunctionDeclaration#snapToGrid. Compaction exacte à conserver: verticalCompactor trie row-col puis remonte y jusqu'au premier blocage avant de pousser récursivement via resolveCompactionCollision; horizontalCompactor trie col-row puis remonte x, et si x + w > cols il wrap sur la ligne suivante. Edge cases obligatoires pour la vague suivante: deltas capteur divisés par transformScale, container scroll conservé dans le calcul initial de position, activationConstraint.distance >= 3 pour fast drag, items static exclus des draggables/sortables, preventCollision conservé pendant resize et drag, et l'ajustement de marge par arrondi dans calcGridItemPosition doit rester visible dans DragOverlay pour éviter les écarts de 0/2px.
